CC1.1.proof: Code of conduct published and acknowledged by all employees annually. Ethics hotline operational. Board oversight of integrity standards documented in governance charter.
CC1.1.notes: Last SOC 2 Type II report confirmed no exceptions.

CC1.2.proof: Board of directors includes independent members with IT/security expertise. Audit committee charter defines oversight responsibilities. Quarterly board review of internal controls.

CC1.3.proof: Organizational chart with reporting lines published. Security team structure documented with clear authorities. RACI matrix for control activities maintained in GRC platform.

CC1.4.proof: Job descriptions include competency requirements for security roles. Annual performance reviews include security objective assessment. Training budget allocated per team.
CC1.4.plan: Implement competency framework with formal skill assessments for security team members.

CC1.5.proof: Internal control responsibilities documented in job descriptions. Control owner accountability tracked in GRC tool. Performance metrics tied to control effectiveness.

CC2.1.proof: Information quality controls implemented across reporting systems. Data validation rules enforced at point of entry. SOC report confirmed information quality controls operating effectively.

CC2.2.proof: Internal communication of control objectives via monthly security newsletter, team meetings, and intranet portal. Policy changes communicated with acknowledgment tracking.

CC2.3.proof: External communication procedures documented for customers, regulators, and auditors. Service status page published. Customer notification procedures for security events established.

CC3.1.proof: Risk management objectives defined and aligned with trust services criteria. Risk appetite statement approved by management. Security objectives documented in service commitments.

CC3.2.proof: Enterprise risk assessment performed annually covering all trust services categories. Risk register maintained with identified risks, likelihood, impact, and risk responses.

CC3.3.proof: Fraud risk assessment conducted annually. Anti-fraud controls documented. Segregation of duties enforced for financial and system administration functions.

CC3.4.proof: Change management process includes risk assessment component. Significant changes evaluated for impact on internal controls. Not all organizational changes trigger reassessment.
CC3.4.plan: Implement automated triggers for control reassessment based on organizational and technology changes.

CC4.1.proof: Ongoing monitoring via automated control testing and SIEM alerting. Separate evaluations through internal audit program. Service auditor assessment performed annually for SOC 2 Type II.

CC4.2.proof: Control deficiency tracking in GRC platform. Remediation plans with target dates assigned to control owners. Monthly reporting to management on open deficiencies.

CC5.1.proof: Control activities mapped to identified risks. Control testing results documented and reviewed. Risk-based approach to control selection validated by service auditor.

CC5.2.proof: General IT controls covering access management, change management, and operations documented and tested. Automated controls monitored continuously. Manual controls tested quarterly.

CC5.3.proof: Control policies published in policy management system. Procedures documented with step-by-step instructions. Annual review cycle ensures currency.

CC6.1.proof: Logical access security architecture documented. Firewall rules reviewed quarterly. IDS/IPS deployed at network boundaries. Cloud security groups configured per least privilege.

CC6.2.proof: User provisioning workflow integrated with HR system. Identity verification performed before credential issuance. Automated deprovisioning on termination. Service auditor confirmed no exceptions.

CC6.3.proof: RBAC model implemented. Access modifications require manager approval. Quarterly user access reviews for all in-scope systems. Segregation of duties enforced in access model.

CC6.4.proof: Physical access controls at data center facilities (badge, biometric). Visitor management system with escort requirements. Access logs retained and reviewed monthly.

CC6.5.proof: Media sanitization procedures following NIST 800-88. Certificates of destruction obtained for disposed hardware. Secure disposal vendor assessed annually.

CC6.6.proof: Perimeter security controls include next-gen firewall, WAF, DDoS protection, and email security gateway. Threat intelligence feeds integrated. SOC monitors external threat indicators.

CC6.7.proof: Data-in-transit encryption enforced (TLS 1.2+). DLP policies monitor data movement. Secure file transfer mechanisms provided for external sharing. USB restrictions enforced.

CC6.8.proof: EDR deployed on all endpoints. Email security with sandboxing for attachments. Application whitelisting on servers. Real-time malware detection with automated quarantine.

CC7.1.proof: Vulnerability scanning weekly (infrastructure) and on-deployment (applications). Configuration drift detection automated. New vulnerability alerts from threat intelligence subscriptions.

CC7.2.proof: SIEM with 24/7 SOC monitoring. UEBA for anomaly detection. Network traffic analysis for malicious patterns. Alert correlation and triage within 15-minute SLA.

CC7.3.proof: Security event evaluation criteria documented. Incident severity classification scheme (P1-P4). Escalation procedures for events meeting incident thresholds.

CC7.4.proof: Incident response plan with playbooks for common incident types. Retainer with external IR firm for surge capacity. Tabletop exercises conducted semi-annually.
CC7.4.notes: Last tabletop exercise: Feb 2026 - ransomware scenario.

CC7.5.proof: Recovery procedures documented for security incidents. Post-incident recovery validation performed. Business continuity plans invoked when incidents impact availability.

CC8.1.proof: Change management process with CAB approval for production changes. Changes tested in staging environment. Rollback procedures documented. Emergency change process with post-implementation review.
CC8.1.notes: Service auditor tested 45 changes with no exceptions noted.

CC9.1.proof: Business continuity and disaster recovery plans documented. DR testing semi-annually for critical systems. RTO/RPO targets defined and validated through testing.

CC9.2.proof: Vendor risk management program with risk-based assessment framework. Critical vendors assessed annually via security questionnaires and SOC report review. Contract provisions for security requirements.
CC9.2.plan: Implement continuous vendor monitoring using security ratings service.

A1.1.proof: Infrastructure capacity monitoring with automated alerting. Auto-scaling configured for cloud workloads. Capacity planning reviews conducted quarterly with growth projections.

A1.2.proof: Environmental controls at data center facilities (fire suppression, HVAC, power). Backup infrastructure with automated failover. Cloud-based DR with multi-region deployment.

A1.3.proof: Recovery plan testing conducted semi-annually. Results documented with identified improvements. Full recovery drill completed for Tier 1 systems only.
A1.3.plan: Extend recovery testing to all in-scope systems. Implement automated recovery testing for cloud workloads.

C1.1.proof: Confidential information classification scheme implemented. Data inventory for confidential data maintained. Access controls enforce need-to-know for confidential information.

C1.2.proof: Secure disposal procedures for confidential information. Automated retention policies enforce deletion schedules. Disposal verification through audit sampling confirmed by service auditor.

PI1.1.proof: Processing specifications documented for each service. Data definitions and processing rules maintained in technical documentation. Service descriptions provided to customers.

PI1.2.proof: Input validation controls implemented across all customer-facing applications. Completeness checks for batch processing. Error handling procedures documented and tested.

PI1.3.proof: Processing controls include data integrity checks, transaction logging, and reconciliation procedures. Automated monitoring for processing anomalies.

PI1.4.proof: Output delivery controls documented. Report distribution restricted to authorized recipients. Timeliness monitoring for scheduled outputs.
PI1.4.plan: Implement automated output validation checks for all customer-facing reports and data feeds.

PI1.5.proof: Data storage controls in place for production systems. Archive and retention procedures documented. Some gaps in tracking items-in-processing across distributed systems.
PI1.5.plan: Implement end-to-end data lineage tracking for processing pipelines.

P1.1.proof: Privacy notice published on website and provided during data collection. Notice updated when privacy practices change. Notification procedures documented for material changes.

P1.2.proof: Privacy notice describes data types collected, purposes, retention periods, disclosure practices, and third-party sharing. Reviewed by legal and DPO quarterly.

P2.1.proof: Consent mechanisms implemented for data collection where required. Opt-in/opt-out choices documented and functional. Consent records maintained with timestamps.

P3.1.proof: Data collection limited to stated purposes per privacy notice. Data minimization principle applied in system design. Privacy impact assessments for new collection activities.

P3.2.proof: Explicit consent workflows implemented for sensitive data collection. Consent forms clearly state consequences of withholding consent. Consent obtained prior to collection.

P4.1.proof: Data use limited to purposes identified in privacy notice. Purpose limitation enforced through access controls and data governance policies. Regular audits verify compliance.

P4.2.proof: Retention schedules defined for all personal information categories. Automated retention policies enforce deletion timelines. Retention compliance monitored quarterly.

P4.3.proof: Secure disposal procedures for personal information. Automated purge processes for expired data. Disposal verification through periodic sampling and audit.

P5.1.proof: Data subject access request (DSAR) process documented. Self-service portal for data access. Requests fulfilled within regulatory timelines. Denial procedures with appeal mechanism.

P5.2.proof: Data correction process exists but relies on manual workflows. Third-party notification of corrections not fully automated.
P5.2.plan: Automate data correction workflows and implement automated notification to downstream third-party recipients.

P6.1.proof: Third-party disclosure requires explicit consent where applicable. Consent verified before disclosure. Disclosure procedures documented with legal review.

P6.2.proof: Disclosure log maintained in data governance platform. All authorized disclosures recorded with date, recipient, purpose, and data categories. Quarterly review by DPO.

P6.3.proof: Breach notification procedures documented. Unauthorized disclosure tracking in incident management system. Breach register maintained with investigation outcomes.

P6.4.proof: Data processing agreements with all vendors handling personal information. Privacy requirements in vendor contracts. Annual compliance assessments for critical data processors.

P6.5.proof: Breach notification clauses in vendor contracts. Not all vendors have been assessed for notification capability. Incident response coordination with vendors is informal.
P6.5.plan: Conduct tabletop exercises with critical data processing vendors to validate notification procedures.

P6.6.proof: Breach notification procedures aligned with regulatory requirements (GDPR 72-hour, state breach notification laws). Communication templates prepared. Legal counsel engaged for all notifications.

P6.7.proof: Data subject accounting requests handled manually. Partial automation for common data categories. Full accounting capability not yet available for all data stores.
P6.7.plan: Implement automated data mapping and accounting capability across all personal information repositories.

P7.1.proof: Data quality controls implemented at collection points. Validation rules for key data fields. Periodic data quality assessments for personal information stores.

P8.1.proof: Privacy complaint handling process documented. Dedicated email for privacy inquiries. Complaints tracked to resolution with SLA. Compliance monitoring includes periodic privacy audits.
